invariant: Every Flutter widget class in lib must be ALREADY_CATALOGED, REPLACE_WITH_EXISTING_CATALOG_ENTRY, PROMOTE_TO_WIDGETBOOK_CATALOG, or DELETE_OR_MERGE_DUPLICATE. KEEP_PRIVATE is invalid.

dashboard_payments_local.knownFalsePositive: lib/payments/presentation/payment_confirmation_screen.dart:469 _QuickActions collides with public QuickActions and should be consolidated, not counted as cataloged.

totalsFromClassifiedShards.note: Dashboard/payments was locally coverage-classified, not fully converted into final classification counts yet. Its 60 rows remain in the first implementation batch with known obvious replacements.

Treat host/event-success as the largest migration batch: 396 widgets, 261 promotions, 88 replacements, 16 delete/merge findings.
groups: User analytics panel shared analytics primitives

implementationNotes: Private Dart widgets cannot be imported from Widgetbook. To catalog them directly, they must be renamed/exported or folded into a public catalog harness in the same library.
Explicit fold/replace/promote/delete decisions live in docs/design_parity/widgetbook_widget_decisions.json and are validated by tool/design/check_widgetbook_coverage.mjs.
The scalable approach is not 907 isolated use cases. It is grouped Widgetbook entries where a screen/section catalog entry owns its private subwidgets as explicit states.

Coverage should ratchet down with check_widgetbook_coverage after each batch. A class leaves the queue when it is cataloged by name, deleted, or replaced by a cataloged parent/primitive and no longer exists as a separate widget class.
